title: Electranthera mutica
intro: Electranthera mutica, also known as Chinese angelica tree, is a perennial plant species native to China, characterized by its large, deciduous leaves and clusters of small white flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Electranthera mutica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal decoctions, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.

Electranthera mutica is a species of plant that is endemic to the Pacific Islands, specifically in the Hawaiian Islands. It is a shrub or small tree that grows in tropical forests and woodlands.
